stories.headline: BTC reclaims $79K as Iran peace proposal flips Fear & Greed from 33 to 47
stories.tldr: Bitcoin pushed back to $79,124 Monday as a weekend Iran peace proposal lifted risk appetite ahead of this week's Fed meeting.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sentiment swung 14 points in a week without new on-chain demand, leaving BTC's bid leaning entirely on macro headlines into FOMC.
the_details: BTC up 2.04% in 24h to $79,123.77; ETH lagged at $2,320.84, failing to retake the $2,400 mark last seen mid-April.
Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 47 (Neutral) Monday, up from 33 last Friday and 29 a week ago.
Total crypto market cap reached $2.71T at 1.7% gain; BTC dominance held at 58.2% with $66.6B in 24h volume.

stories.headline: Crypto exploit losses top $606M in first 24 days of April, Lazarus fingerprints on Drift
stories.tldr: April is now the worst month for crypto exploits in 2026, with bridge failures and admin breaches dominating $606M+ in losses.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two state-tied attacks inside 19 days have repriced DeFi smart-contract risk and could chill institutional bridge volume into Q3.
the_details: Drift Protocol's April 1 $285M hit was traced to a North Korean-linked social-engineering campaign using pre-signed hidden authorizations.
Kelp DAO's April 19 LayerZero bridge exploit drained 116,500 rsETH ($292M), about 18% of the token's circulating supply.
Volo Protocol followed with a $3.5M loss April 22, the third major April exploit inside a four-week stretch.

stories.headline: $4.5B at risk as researchers flag half of LayerZero apps still vulnerable post-Kelp
stories.tldr: Researchers warn nearly 50% of active LayerZero-powered apps remain exposed to the same vector that drained Kelp DAO's bridge.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A single shared attack surface across $4.5B of TVL turns LayerZero from a connectivity layer into the market's biggest unhedged tail risk.
the_details: Aave saw $8.4B in deposits flee in 48 hours after the Kelp exploit, with total DeFi TVL down more than $13B network-wide.
The Kelp attacker exploited LayerZero's bridge logic to mint and sweep wrapped rsETH across 20 chains simultaneously.
No coordinated patch has shipped; teams are choosing between manual freezes and waiting for LayerZero guidance.

stories.headline: Aave bridges $14.7B TVL strategy onto Solana via Sunrise DeFi deployment
stories.tldr: Aave is routing Ethereum-backed liquidity into Solana through Sunrise DeFi, betting on faster execution and lower fees to ignite cross-chain demand.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Aave's first real Solana foothold pressures Kamino and MarginFi at exactly the moment Solana's $5.6B DeFi base needs new institutional flows.
the_details: Ethereum still anchors over $12B of Aave's $14.7B TVL, but new deposits are now routable to Solana markets.
Solana's Alpenglow upgrade is already live with sub-200ms finality; Firedancer aims to push throughput from ~1,300 to 1M TPS.
Move comes as new Aave borrow demand stalls on mainnet despite the lending protocol's record-high deposit base.

stories.headline: Spot Solana ETF inflows collapse 92% to $34M in April, down from $419M November peak
stories.tldr: Institutional appetite for SOL spot products has dried up alongside a five-month price slide and a confirmed death cross on the daily chart.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: ETF flow has been the marginal SOL bid since launch — losing it leaves price discovery to retail and miners just as Firedancer hype peaks.
the_details: SOL trades near $86.47 with a $49.8B market cap, ranking seventh among crypto assets and clinging to its 50-day MA at $85.30.
Daily chart shows a death cross with the 200-day MA at $131.20, its first since the 2024 cycle bottom.
Spot SOL ETFs absorbed just $34M in April vs. $419M in November 2025, a 92% drop across a six-month window.
